description: ML-ready label schema for recommending top 3-5 study fields from questionnaire responses.

sample_id.description: Unique identifier for one training example (typically one completed questionnaire session).
rules: Must be unique within a dataset split.

questionnaire_version.description: Version of questionnaire that produced the responses.

responses.description: Map of question_id to normalized answer payload.
rules: Question IDs must match source question bank IDs.
For Likert5, value must be integer in [1,5].

true_fields.description: Ground-truth relevant fields for this sample.
rules: All values must be in canonical_fields.

primary_field.description: Single strongest field label for compatibility with single-label analyses.

rules: Must contain all canonical fields as keys.

top_k_fields.description: Fields sorted by descending score used as recommendation output.
rules: k must be between 3 and 5 inclusive.
Values must be unique and from canonical_fields.

label_quality.description: Trust level for this training row.

rules: If label_source == synthetic_augmented, is_synthetic must be 1.

taxonomy_version.description: Taxonomy version used to label this row.
rules: Must match the canonical taxonomy release (currently v1).

created_at_utc.description: Creation timestamp in ISO-8601 UTC format.

global_rules: Do not use non-canonical field names.
Do not mix multiple taxonomy versions in one training split without explicit migration.
Do not train only on synthetic data.
Preserve label_source and label_quality for every row.
Use deterministic preprocessing and fixed random seeds for reproducibility.
